explanation: The specification states that at the end of every inspiration phase (assuming this is signaled by some conjunction or situation, but since we only have prop_0 and prop_1, we are told that the end of an inspiration phase is characterized by both prop_0 and prop_1 being true), then prop_2 must happen. In LTL, this means that globally, whenever prop_0 & prop_1 occurs, then prop_2 holds at the same time or immediately afterward. This is written as: globally, if prop_0 & prop_1 are true, then eventually (immediately or in the future) prop_2 must be true. But since the English says "at the end", it's likely they mean immediately: so we must have that at every point, if prop_0 & prop_1, then prop_2. That is: G((prop_0 & prop_1) -> prop_2)

The statement indicates that at the time when prop_0 holds, if both prop_1 and prop_2 also hold, then prop_3 should occur (which can be interpreted as either 'now' or 'immediately after' since LTL's semantics can vary, but the most natural is to use implication at that point). Thus, the formula globally enforces: whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 and prop_2 together imply prop_3.

The requirement says that at the end of an inspiration phase, if both prop_0 and prop_1 hold, then prop_2 occurs/is triggered. Since LTL does not have explicit phase markers, we interpret 'at the end of an inspiration phase' as 'whenever (prop_0 & prop_1) becomes true, prop_2 holds at that moment'. So, globally, whenever both prop_0 and prop_1 hold, prop_2 holds.

The sentence states that at the time when prop_0 holds, if both prop_1 and prop_2 also hold at that point, then prop_3 must hold. This can be expressed as: whenever (prop_0 & prop_1 & prop_2), then prop_3. Globally, at any point, if prop_0 & prop_1 & prop_2 holds, then prop_3 must also hold at that point.

This statement indicates that at moments when prop_0 is true, if both prop_1 and prop_2 are also true, then prop_3 should occur (be true) at that time. This is a safety property that must hold globally: whenever prop_0 & prop_1 & prop_2, then prop_3.

The requirement states that at the moment where prop_0 holds, if both prop_1 and prop_2 hold as well, then prop_3 must hold at that point. This can be represented in LTL as: globally, at every point, if prop_0 is true, then (if prop_1 and prop_2 are true, prop_3 must also be true at that same moment).

The requirement states that at the position (or time point) when prop_0 holds, if both prop_1 and prop_2 also hold, then prop_3 must hold as well. This can be formalized such that globally, if all of prop_0, prop_1, and prop_2 are true at a state, then prop_3 must also be true at that same state.

The sentence specifies a property at the end of an inspiration phase, which is left implicit. Because there is no explicit atomic proposition for 'end of inspiration phase', we represent the requirement as: whenever (prop_0 & prop_1 & prop_2) holds, that indicates it is the end of the inspiration phase. If further outputs or actions were specified upon this condition, they would be encoded as implications. Since no action is specified, the LTL simply indicates recognition of the condition.

This requirement states that at the end of every inspiration phase, if both prop_0 and prop_1 hold at that time, then prop_2 should also hold. Assuming that 'the end of the inspiration phase' is an implicitly recurring condition in the model (not directly captured by a proposition here), we can formalize the requirement as: globally, if at any time prop_0 and prop_1 both hold, then prop_2 must also hold at that time.

The requirement says that at the end of every inspiration phase, if both prop_0 and prop_1 hold, then prop_2 must hold (presumably at that moment or as an action taken). Without explicit phase representation, we translate the implication so that globally, whenever the end of the inspiration phase occurs and both prop_0 and prop_1 are true, then prop_2 must also be true at that point. Since we have no explicit phase indicator, we only encode the implication for the atomic propositions.
